what_it_does: This file is a ledger noticeboard item that documents the substrate-mcp toolset, its source location, installed launchers, architecture (three coupled stores), and its relevance to the company runtime's address-graph-over-markdown approach.
observations: substrate-mcp is sourced from /home/tim/repos/obsidian-overlord/ and installed at ~/.local/bin/
Exposed as the substrate-mcp__* MCP toolset with 90+ tools including search_semantic, get_by_address, traverse_links, get_neighborhood, get_vault_schema, cluster_by_embedding, add_vault, reindex
Architecture has three coupled stores: address-graph SQLite, autopoietic type-graph SQLite, and a Chroma semantic face using local Ollama embeddings
Kept fresh by a filesystem watcher with resumable scans and sha256 skip
Parses wikilinks and frontmatter into a typed graph
The file argues this is structurally the same shape as the company runtime's board:// resolver over channel-memory/noticeboard/*.md
Recommends studying the substrate-mcp parser before re-deriving a bridge
standouts: substrate-mcp already implements an address+type+semantic graph projected over a markdown vault
The file explicitly frames this as convergent with the company runtime's board:// markdown resolver
It treats markdown as canonical truth and derived stores as projections
conventions: Uses ledger frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses markdown headings and bullet lists
References board:// items in frontmatter links
concerns: The title contains an evaluative superlative that is not substantiated inside the file body
The duplication/convergence claim is asserted but not evidenced from this file alone
notes: A ledger note arguing that substrate-mcp is an existing local implementation of the same address-graph-over-markdown pattern the company runtime uses, and that its parser is worth studying.
questions: What is the exact technical relationship or integration path between substrate-mcp and the company runtime board resolver?
Which specific 90+ tools are included beyond the examples listed?
How does the filesystem watcher handle concurrent edits or conflicts?
What Ollama embedding model and Chroma configuration are used?
Is there a planned bridge or is this purely a recommendation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe substrate-mcp and its architectural relevance; the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: projection stores with markdown as canonical truth
intention_signals: It appears to be making the case that substrate-mcp is a relevant precedent for the company runtime's markdown address graph and that its parser should be examined before creating a new bridge.
novelty: It frames an existing local tool as an architectural precedent for the company runtime, highlighting convergence rather than introducing a new component.
contribution: Records the existence, location, launcher paths, and architecture of substrate-mcp; captures the insight that a local address+type+semantic graph over markdown already exists; flags a parser-study opportunity for any future bridge work.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard ledger item describing the substrate-mcp address-graph MCP toolset, its three-store architecture over a markdown vault, and its structural similarity to the company runtime's board:// markdown resolver.
intention_for_embedding: To flag substrate-mcp as existing prior art and recommend studying its parser before deriving a new bridge between markdown and the company runtime address graph.
